name: Stowe
terrain_notes: Classic Vermont terrain with strong tree skiing, steeps, and polished groomers.
best_for: Epic pass holders who want a high-quality Northeast trip with trees and a premium village feel.
avoid_if: You need the lowest lodging cost or want a short, low-key day trip.
trip_tips: Start early on weekend powder days and plan extra time for mountain road traffic.
lodging_notes: Lodging is often pricier near the mountain; nearby towns can offer better value.

name: Killington
terrain_notes: Large trail network with strong groomers, terrain parks, and enough variety for mixed groups.
best_for: Ikon pass holders who want lots of acreage and nightlife options.
avoid_if: You strongly prefer compact, quiet resorts.
trip_tips: Use the size of the resort to move around when popular pods get crowded.
lodging_notes: Many lodging options sit along the access road with shuttle or short-drive access.

name: Mount Snow
terrain_notes: Known for terrain parks and accessible groomers with moderate tree options.
best_for: Epic pass holders looking for a closer Vermont option from Boston.
avoid_if: Deep trees and expert natural snow are the main priority.
trip_tips: Park-focused riders should check Carinthia conditions first.
lodging_notes: Nearby inns and condos can keep costs lower than premium Vermont destinations.

name: Okemo
terrain_notes: Smooth groomers and approachable cruising terrain are the main strengths.
best_for: Epic pass holders who prefer polished groomers and a mellow trip.
avoid_if: You want steep, technical trees or a strong park scene.
trip_tips: Good choice for mixed ability groups and lower-drama ski days.
lodging_notes: Ludlow has convenient lodging and dining near the mountain.

name: Wildcat
terrain_notes: Old-school New Hampshire feel with strong views, natural terrain, and tree pockets.
best_for: Epic pass holders who want a rugged Northeast day with stronger trees.
avoid_if: You need extensive base amenities or lots of beginner-friendly polish.
trip_tips: Check wind holds and weather exposure before committing.
lodging_notes: Stay in the Mount Washington Valley for more lodging and food options.

name: Attitash
terrain_notes: Varied New Hampshire terrain with approachable groomers and some tree options.
best_for: Epic pass holders building a practical Mount Washington Valley trip.
avoid_if: You are chasing the strongest expert terrain in the region.
trip_tips: Pair with Wildcat if conditions favor a multi-mountain trip.
lodging_notes: Bartlett and North Conway provide flexible lodging choices.

name: Loon
terrain_notes: Accessible New Hampshire resort with strong parks, groomers, and convenient layout.
best_for: Ikon pass holders who want a shorter drive and efficient weekend logistics.
avoid_if: You want remote, uncrowded powder skiing.
trip_tips: Arrive early on weekends because convenience can mean crowds.
lodging_notes: Lincoln offers practical lodging close to the resort.

name: Sunday River
terrain_notes: Broad Maine resort with many pods, reliable groomers, and moderate tree/park options.
best_for: Ikon pass holders who want variety across a multi-day Northeast trip.
avoid_if: You need the shortest possible drive from Boston.
trip_tips: Move between peaks to find better snow and shorter lift lines.
lodging_notes: On-mountain and Bethel-area lodging both work well for multi-day trips.

name: Sugarloaf
terrain_notes: Big-mountain Maine feel with stronger expert terrain, trees, and wind-exposed upper mountain zones.
best_for: Ikon pass holders willing to drive farther for a more serious Northeast mountain.
avoid_if: You need easy travel logistics or mild weather exposure.
trip_tips: Watch upper mountain wind and snow reports before choosing ski days.
lodging_notes: On-mountain lodging is convenient because the resort is remote.

name: Jay Peak
terrain_notes: Tree-heavy Vermont resort known for natural snow and glades.
best_for: Skiers prioritizing trees and powder over pass alignment.
avoid_if: You need Epic or Ikon pass value or polished groomer-first terrain.
trip_tips: Best when recent snow supports glade coverage.
lodging_notes: Lodging is more limited nearby, so book early for storm cycles.

name: Vail
terrain_notes: Huge Colorado resort with expansive bowls, groomers, trees, and premium infrastructure.
best_for: Epic pass holders planning a longer Western destination trip.
avoid_if: You have a short trip from Boston or a tight lodging budget.
trip_tips: Use back bowls timing carefully and expect premium destination logistics.
lodging_notes: Lodging is expensive near the village; nearby towns may reduce cost.

name: Breckenridge
terrain_notes: High-alpine Colorado resort with excellent park options, groomers, and varied bowls.
best_for: Epic pass holders who care about park, town energy, and broad terrain choice.
avoid_if: You want quiet lodging or low crowds during peak travel periods.
trip_tips: Altitude can matter; build in adjustment time for longer trips.
lodging_notes: Town lodging is convenient but often pricey in peak season.

name: Park City
terrain_notes: Large Utah resort with extensive groomers, park options, and easy town access.
best_for: Epic pass holders who want destination skiing with strong non-ski amenities.
avoid_if: You are mostly chasing steep powder terrain.
trip_tips: Use the large footprint to spread out and avoid bottlenecks.
lodging_notes: Staying near town gives good dining and transit convenience.

name: Snowbird
terrain_notes: Steep Utah terrain with major powder upside and strong expert appeal.
best_for: Ikon pass holders who prioritize powder and challenging terrain.
avoid_if: You prefer mellow groomers or beginner-heavy group trips.
trip_tips: Storm days can be excellent, but terrain intensity and visibility matter.
lodging_notes: Canyon lodging is convenient but limited; Salt Lake lodging can reduce cost.

name: Winter Park
terrain_notes: Colorado resort with strong trees, bumps, groomers, and a practical Front Range feel.
best_for: Ikon pass holders looking for terrain variety without the most premium lodging costs.
avoid_if: You want the shortest travel from Boston for a 3-day trip.
trip_tips: Explore Mary Jane if trees and bumps are priorities.
lodging_notes: Town and nearby lodging can be more budget-friendly than marquee Colorado resorts.

name: Steamboat
terrain_notes: Colorado resort known for trees, softer snow, and a welcoming destination feel.
best_for: Ikon pass holders who want trees and powder on a longer Western trip.
avoid_if: You need short-trip efficiency from the East Coast.
trip_tips: Tree-focused skiers should prioritize storm cycles and visibility windows.
lodging_notes: Destination lodging can add up, but town options provide variety.
